Report of 5 months exchange semester at ITMO university in Saint-Petersburg Preface

Report of 5 months exchange semester at ITMO university in Saint-Petersburg ALEXIS CARBILLET 2020-01-19 Preface Welcome! In this website, I will share the experience that I lived during my exchange semester in ITMO university. I will also describe what I learnt and how it will be useful in my professional life. This report is also available in pdf. All the work I did during this semester use the language Python. Also, all the classes were in english, not in russian.

Acknowledgments First of all, I thank my school, IMT Mines Alès, for having the partnership with ITMO University. I also thank them for letting me go there during a semester. Then, I thank ITMO University for welcoming me in Saint-Petersburg and participating to my education. Finally, I thank the professors for sharing their knowledge with me.
Introduction Why did I choose Russia? About Saint-Petersburg About ITMO University

Introduction From the 1st of September 2019 to the 31st of January 2020 I did an exchange semester at ITMO University. In this introduction, I will first explain why, as a French, I chose to go in Russia. Then, I will talk about Saint-Petersburg and finally about the university. Why did I choose Russia? At first, french people, and other people, can find this choice surprising. I think so most surprised were the russian students. As an european student of data science, the expected choice is United States, Japan, China… but not Russia. Russian students want to go to Europe so they don’t understand why european student go to their country. So why this choice? I studied, during my two first year of engineering school, russian. I wanted to improve it and it was the opportunity to do it. This is not the only reason. I am curious and I like travelling. In Europe, Russia has an image of rudeness and strong government. If you study the french history, you will notice that France and Russia have been allied during a lot of time. Some of the russian tsars were in love with France. They also defeated our great french emperor Napoleon 1st while France was dominating Europe from 1804 to 1814. They also fight with my country in the two world war. This participated to my decision. I decided to study artificial intelligence and data science. Russia is one of the countries investing the most in artificial intelligence. Vladimir Putin has determined that “whoever becomes the leader in this field will become the master of the world” (Rapport de mission parlementaire, 2018, C.Villani). In fact, most of the russian think that the technology is the most important axis to be developped in their country. This semester was an opportunity to meet those people who might have an important job in this country later. About Saint-Petersburg 5millions people live in this city, which is 10 times bigger than Paris. This difference of area is explained by the fact that there is no skyscraper in the city. This city the cultural capital of Russia. It was the capital of the country before the creation of the republic. There are around 250 museums and more than 40 theaters. It contains one of the biggest museum of the world, the hermitage museum. Its metro is one of the deepest of the world because of the rivers inside the city (mainly the Neva). It is also considered the most european city of Russia according to the numerous russian I talked to (most of them were from others russian cities, Moscow included). It is the second biggest city of Russia by its population, after Moscow where 12 millions people live. It is also is the second city in Europe by its surface and the fourth by its population within city limits according to Wikipedia. I decided to go to this city instead of Moscow because the universities in Moscow only teach in russian according to the agreements they have with my school. About ITMO University ITMO University is one Russia’s leading higher education and research institutions, specializing in Information Technology, Optical Design and Engineering. It have many buildings in different part of the city. Classes can be held until 10pm and on Saturday too which is not the case in France. The university consists of 18 departments, 7 research institutes and one academy, with a total of 119 chairs. As of April 1, 2014, the total number of students is 13,890, with over 900 being foreign nationals. Their team won 7 times the International Collegiate Programming Contest and did a lot fo second and third place since 1999 which shows its high level in computer science. ITMO University has three museums: “Museum of ITMO University History”, “Museum of the Institute of Refrigeration and Biotechnologies” and “Museum of Optics”. In the next part, I will describe the classes I followed, what I learn and why I decided to followed this class. It will enhance the skills I developped during this semester.

Chapter 1 Negociation Negotiation is a process of communication and exchange between at least two parties, the object of which concerns the organization of a relationship or the settlement of a problem between them. Each class was composed of a theorical part at the beginning. Then a practical part was done, in small group. Its goal was to apply the knowledge given during the first part. There was negociation between two parties, three parties and at the final class, a negociation between six parties has been organized. The context was different each time. For example, it could be a customer who had a problem with the product he/she just bought, an agent who tried to get a contract for his actor or a problem between two departments of a company and its direction. It was interesting because each parties had different information so it was not possible to be totally prepare for the negociation, as in real life. All the negociatiions were in english. In total, more than 8 negociations have been done. Different kind of negociations have been taught. First the Hardvard school of negociation, then the Kremlin school of negation and finally the Swedish school of negociation. Some books needed to be read like “Getting to Yes” by Roger Fisher and William L. Ury and also “Predictably Irrational: The Hidden Forces That Shape Our Decisions” by Dan Ariely. I discovered the concept of Zone of possible agreement (ZOPA) as well as the Best alternative to negotiated agreement (BATNA). I also learn how to profile people according to 4 categories (Dominant, Influencer, Compliant and Steady).

Chapter 2 Social Media Data Analysis Each class was about a topic linked to social media and the use of their data. Here are the list of the topic that have been presented: text classification micro blog sentiment analysis spam detection event detection graph theory multi source data fusion mobility and demography knowledge graph Two little projects have been done during those classes. The first one was about sentiment analysis and the other one about Demography Prediction. Finally, a bigger project have been done. Data have been scraped from Youtube, Reddit and Twitter. After processing, a sentiment analysis has been done for each social media. The goal was to rank the president and the senators of United States of America by popularity. The main problem was to get comments on Youtube videos because sometimes senators don’t allow them so the calculus of their popularity was harder. It could have been improved with a spam detection and a graph of connection between the different users of those platform in order to detect communities.

Chapter 3 Image processsing In this class, numerous application of image processing have been shown. For example, there were pyramid blending (mix two images in one) and potential methods. Then some theory about using neural network in image processing have been presented (segmentation, object detection,…). Here are the projects in link with this topic: Machine learning with images Image classification with CNN Image segmentation with SegNet Object detection with Retinanet

Chapter 4 Evolutionnary computing This class was based on the book “Introduction to Evolutionary Computing” by A.E. Eiben and J.E. Smith. The concepts of genetic algorithm as well as evolutionary computing have been explained. There was also an introduction to the particle swarm algorithm, gravitation search algorithm and harmony search. Some projects have been conducted such as the Travelling Salesman Problem.

Chapter 5 Speech recognition This class was composed of 5 lectures. Those were about: introduction to speech recognition (metrics, definitions) features from a speech waveform acoustic modeling language modeling speech decoding The first, the second and the fourth lectures included practical assignments.

Chapter 6 Data Warehousing This class was based on the book “The Data Warehouse Toolkit” by Ralph Kimball and Margy Ross. After each class, an assignment was given (procedures between tables and databases, Kimball architecture, Inmon architecture, indexes,…). It used the language transact SQL (it is used in Microsoft Azure).

Chapter 7 Information Retrieval Each class was about a topic linked to information retrieval. Here are the list of the topic that have been presented: Boolean Retrieval Model (Index) Spellchecker and Suggestions Antispam and Antiporn Text Ranking Machine Learning in ranking Neural networks in ranking Some kaggle competitions have been organized about Antispam, Indexation, Spellchecker and Ranking. This class was done by people from Mail.ru

Chapter 8 Recommender System This class was organized by an employee of Yandex. A lot of different recommender systems have been explained (Calibrated recommender, Youtube recommender, Restricted Boltzmann Machine,…). Two kaggle competitions have been done: one with explicit feedback (ratings) and another one with implicit feedback. I did a project for this class, it is a movie recommender which works offline.

Conclusion During this semester, I learnt how to conduct a negociation. It helped me to improve my english and russian speaking. I also leanrt about genetic algorithm and evolutionnary computing. I developped my knowledge in image processing and in data analysis from social media (twitter, reddit, youtube). Many applications of data science have been showed in Information Retrieval and Recommender System classes. I also followed classes which the topic were speech recogintion and data warehousing. All the projects from computer science classes used the language Python.

Abstract Report of the exchange semester at ITMO university by Alexis Carbillet Those 5 months allowed me to develop my soft skills like my russian, my english or my negociation skills. My hard skills have also been improved. I discovered how to use deep neural network for image processing and what was evolutionnary computing. I am now aware of more application of data science in the industry. This knowledge will undoubtedly be useful in my career as a data scientist. Keywords: exchange semester, ITMO university, artificial intelligence, data science
